title: Unit 3 Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass: Being and Having

script.text: Welcome... to the Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass for Unit 3: Being and Having. To build real sentences in Greek... you need verbs. And if you only ever learn two verbs... they should be
for 'To Have'. They are the keys to a thousand conversations. Athina... where do we start with these building blocks?
Before we meet the verbs... we must meet the actors. The Personal Pronouns. This is how we say 'I'... 'You'... 'He'... and so on. Let's learn them clearly.
Read the singular ones first... the actors on stage by themselves.
and his friends share the same endings as the nouns we learned in Unit 2...
It’s all very consistent. Now... what about the groups? The 'We'... the 'You all'... and 'They'?
for you. And for they... we have
If those sound confusing... just remember they rhyme perfectly.
Now... this next point is one of the biggest 'Masterclass' tips. The 'Pro-Drop' rule. In English... we HAVE to say 'I' am a student. But in Greek? Athina?
In Greek... we drop the pronoun 90% of the time! You don't need
because the ending of the verb already tells everyone it is YOU. If you say
you are being very emphatic... like saying... 'I specifically am the one'. Normally... just say
Drop the pronouns to sound like a native. Got it. Now... let's conjugate our two champions. First:
to be. This is used for identity... location... and states of being. Athina... take it away.
was both 'he is' AND 'they are'? That's a 'Caution' in our lesson.
handles both. You will always know which one it is because of the context... and because of the nouns that follow it. Don't worry... it becomes natural very fast.
to have. Possession... age... family. This one is very regular but has a 'street' secret.
Έχω... έχεις... έχει. Έχουμε... έχετε... έχουν.
But in the street... we often add an extra 'e' sound to
when you're talking with friends. Now... what about the 'Respect' system? In English, 'You' is always 'You'. But in Greek... if I'm talking to my boss... or a stranger?
Even if you are only talking to ONE person. It’s like 'Usted' in Spanish or 'Vous' in French. It shows respect and distance.
And finally... asking a question. How do we turn 'You have the book' into 'Do you have the book?'
No helper words! No 'Do' or 'Does'. Just raise your voice at the end.
And remember... write it with a semicolon!

You have successfully completed the Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass for Unit 3. You now have the verbs to say who you are... what you have... and how to treat a stranger with respect. Review the everyday objects and places vocabulary to test your
skills. Excellent work... we’ll see you in Unit 4: The Engine Room of Greek Verbs.
Συγχαρητήρια! Τα λέμε στο επόμενο μάθημα.
